prose: Fukui reactivity indices for aniline (C₆H₅NH₂, 14 atoms) computed at DFT tier 'standard' = B3LYP/def2-TZVP (PySCF RKS, density-fit RI), gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 1. N±1 states: cation charge +1 mult 2, anion charge −1 mult 2. Partial charges by Mulliken scheme (PySCF). Geometry: user-supplied aniline.xyz, used as-is (single-point, not re-optimized at DFT). All three SCFs converged. Integrity trustworthy=true. Fukui charge conservation checks passed (Σf⁺ = Σf⁻ = 1.000). No warnings reported. Headline value reported is f⁻ (nucleophilic Fukui) on N (atom 1) = 0.20452359, the most nucleophilic site. Most electrophilic site: C-ortho (atom 3), f⁺ = 2.2975867. The result is chemically consistent with aniline's known nitrogen-centered nucleophilicity and ortho/para-directing character in electrophilic aromatic substitution.
